clues.title: Dr Hargrove's Experiment Log
clues.description: A meticulous laboratory notebook documenting six months of experiments in electromagnetic resonance. The final entries describe anomalous readings that correspond to no known physical phenomenon — frequencies that appear to originate from outside the measurable spectrum.

clues.title: The Anomalous Readings
clues.description: Instrument readings from Hargrove's apparatus that defy rational explanation. The electromagnetic detector registers signals at frequencies that should not exist — and the signals form patterns that resemble Grey Dawn summoning notation.

clues.title: The Hidden Grey Dawn Text
clues.description: Concealed beneath Hargrove's workbench, a Grey Dawn treatise on 'The Electromagnetic Properties of the Veil.' Hargrove has been cross-referencing occult theory with scientific measurement — and finding correlations that neither discipline can explain alone.

clues.title: The Circle's Suppression Order
clues.description: A letter from the Rationalists Circle leadership ordering Hargrove to cease his experiments and destroy his notes. The letter cites 'reputational risk to the Circle' but the subtext is clear: Hargrove's results threaten the Circle's foundational commitment to rational materialism.

clues.title: Professor Crane's Jealousy
clues.description: Professor Crane, a rival scientist, has been publicly critical of Hargrove's work and was seen near the laboratory the night the apparatus was damaged. Suspicious — but Crane's criticism is purely academic, and the damage was caused by the anomalous readings themselves, not sabotage.
